enonce.texte: Read the sentences below carefully. Each sentence describes a situation related to volunteerism. Choose the correct word or expression from the options to complete each sentence.

questions.texte: A person who freely offers to do something without being paid is called a ________.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify the correct term: (a) worker (b) volunteer (c) employer (d) customer

questions.texte: The main feeling of unity and cooperation between people who have the same interests is called ________.

sousQuestions.texte: Select the appropriate word: (a) conflict (b) competition (c) solidarity (d) indifference

questions.texte: To 'commit' to a cause means to ________.

sousQuestions.texte: Which definition best matches 'commitment'? (a) To ignore a problem (b) To promise and dedicate oneself to a cause (c) To get paid for a service (d) To travel for leisure
sousQuestions.reponse: (b) To promise and dedicate oneself to a cause

questions.texte: The action of working together with a group of people to achieve a common goal is known as ________ work.

sousQuestions.texte: Complete the expression: (a) personal (b) selfish (c) community (d) secret

enonce.texte: Read the case study about a community in Cameroon and answer the following questions. Use the vocabulary and concepts from Lesson 47 (volunteer, solidarity, commitment, community development).
images: A picture of a rural Cameroonian community during a volunteer-led clean-up campaign. People of different ages are working together, some are sweeping, others are planting trees near a primary school. The atmosphere is collaborative and cheerful.

questions.texte: Case Study: The village of Nkolbisson is facing a severe waste management problem. The government does not have enough funds to clean the main drainage channels before the rainy season. A group of youths decides to organize a weekend clean-up campaign. They invite all residents to participate for free.
questions.reponse: The youths are demonstrating volunteerism because they are offering their service freely without expecting payment to solve a community problem.

sousQuestions.texte: Justify why the action of the youths in Nkolbisson can be described as 'volunteerism'.
sousQuestions.reponse: This is volunteerism because the youths are acting voluntarily, without financial gain, to address a public health issue (waste management) that affects their community. Their motivation is solidarity and community well-being.

sousQuestions.texte: If you were the leader of this youth group, propose two specific strategies to demonstrate 'commitment' and ensure the project's success beyond just one weekend.
sousQuestions.reponse: Strategy 1: Create a rotating schedule where different community members take turns monitoring the drainage channels every week to prevent new waste accumulation. Strategy 2: Organize an educational awareness campaign in the local school and market to teach people about proper waste disposal, ensuring long-term behavioral change.

questions.texte: Based on the same case study, a local chief criticizes the initiative, saying: "If there is no money, there is no work. Let the government hire paid workers."
questions.reponse: Voluntary work is valuable because it addresses immediate needs, builds social solidarity, and achieves results faster than waiting for government funding.

sousQuestions.texte: Evaluate the chief's statement. Provide one argument explaining why voluntary work is still valuable in this situation.
sousQuestions.reponse: The chief's statement is limited because it ignores the power of community solidarity. Voluntary work is valuable here because waiting for government funding could take months, while the rainy season is imminent. Volunteerism allows the community to act immediately, preventing potential flooding and diseases, while also strengthening social bonds and a sense of shared responsibility.

questions.texte: Match each motivation for volunteerism with its correct example from the case study.

sousQuestions.texte: 2. Personal Skill Development --- (b) Avoiding a fine from the council
3. Community Well-being --- (c) Joining because you want to help your neighbours avoid flooding
